Off Calendar Jun-03-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Drew Lewis, Esq., DREW LEWIS, PC, (833) 600-7400, drew@drewlewis.law for Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

Matter is not contested/argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Farhoud Madani'S Motion To Compel Deposition Of Person Most Knowledgeable And For Sanctions In The Amount Of $3,255.85 Jointly And Severally Against Defendant And His Counsel Joel Glaser.

Plaintiff seeks an order compelling Defendant to produce its PMK (Mr. Ruffino) for further deposition and for sanctions. Plaintiff was able to take Defendant's PMK deposition on 2/22/22 but only for a short period of time due to Mr. Ruffino's scheduling limitations. The parties rescheduled Mr. Ruffino's continued deposition to 3/3/22. However, due to a work emergency, Mr. Ruffino did not appear at the continued deposition on 3/3/22. After the filing of this motion, the parties agreed to complete Mr. Ruffino's deposition on 4/22/22. Plaintiff subsequently took the 4/22/22 deposition off calendar due to the discovery dispute concerning the production of the emails that were the subject of Plaintiff's first motion. In order to ensure that the deposition of Defendant's PMK concludes in a timely manner, Plaintiff's motion to compel is granted and Defendant must produce Mr. Ruffino for deposition no later than 6/20/22. All requests for sanctions are denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, attach a copy of the report and recommendation. Compliance with CRC 3.1312 is required.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/SRB)

Plaintiff Farhoud Madani'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Plaintiff'S Demand For Production (Discovery Non-Stip)
Off Calendar Jun-03-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Drew Lewis, Esq., DREW LEWIS, PC, (833) 600-7400, drew@drewlewis.law for Plaintiff.
No other appearances.

Matter is not contested/argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Farhoud Madani'S Motion To Compel Compliance With Plaintiff'S Demand For Production.

Plaintiff seeks an order compelling Defendant to produce emails in their native format. Defendant failed to follow the instructions in the inspection demand specifying that all emails be produced in the native format (or in a usable electronic format that preserves all metadata) and produced the emails in PDF format instead. Defendant did not object to the format instructions or claim burden/expense associated with the production in its response to the inspection demand. Defendant raised that objection belatedly during the meet and confer process and in opposition to this motion. Defendant's objection was therefore waived. Accordingly, Plaintiff is entitled to receive the emails in question in either the native format or in a usable electronic format that preserves all metadata. Defendant is ordered to produce the emails in their native format no later than Friday, June 10, 2022. Request for sanctions is denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, attach a copy of the report and recommendation. Compliance with CRC 3.1312 is required.

Judge: Suzanne Bolanos, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/SRB)
